Ntc Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order
Off Calendar Oct-15-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS RUBIN SALEM, ZAHIRA SALEM, AND MUHAMMED SALEM'S MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER. Appearances: Samantha Wolff and Jahmal Davis of Hanson Bridgett (995.5020/995.5815) for defendants and moving parties; Paul Wartelle (no phone number given) for plaintiffs and responding parties. Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Granted. The court adopts defendants' order, except requests for sanctions denied, each side acted with substantial justification. Order signed. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Not reported

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Pltf'S First Set Of Special Interrogatories, Requests For Admission And Form Interrogatories
Off Calendar Oct-15-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFFS JUDITH ROGERS, DELCIA CARTER MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFF'S FIRST SET OF SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION AND FORM INTERROGATORIES. Appearances: Samantha Wolff and Jahmal Davis of Hanson Bridgett (995.5020/995.5815) for defendants and responding parties; Paul Wartelle (no phone number given) for plaintiffs and moving parties. Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: denied. Plaintiffs are not entitled to discovery that does not relate to their individual claims and exceeds the proper scope of discovery. This action is not pled as a class action. The San Francisco minimum wage ordinance, which references a "person...acting on behalf of the public as provided for under applicable state law" is similar to the unfair competition law. The ordinance is dissimilar to the labor code private attorneys general act of 2004 which allows an aggrieved employee to bring a civil action personally and on behalf of other employees to recover civil penalties, with 75 percent going to the labor and workforce development agency and 25 percent to the aggrieved employees. Request for sanctions denied. Order signed. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Not reported
